spec_match.rationale: Covers part of the spec (1 of 4 named API surfaces) — distinct angle worth picking up.

interactive.rationale: Real controls + script-driven side-effects make this hands-on, not a static card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Most of the blurb's promises land but a few of the named behaviours aren't shown.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is covered but only a few failure modes are surfaced.

references.rationale: Page does not cite the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> reference link — required self-heal anchor.

design_system.rationale: Has 2 raw hex colour value(s) inline — should use --bg-*/--text-*/--accent-* tokens instead.

openQuestions.title: Surface more named spec API for corner-shaping-corner-shape-superellipse-squircle
openQuestions.detail: The spec summary lists API surfaces this concept doesn't currently name on-page. Either rename the controls / readouts to mention them, or add a second concept page that targets the missing surface directly. The reviewer extracted these from the spec: CSS Borders 4 corner-shaping. New: corner-shape property accepts round | square | bevel | scoop | notch | squircle | superellipse(<number>). Per-corner: corner-top-left-shape etc. Shorthand: corner-shape with 1-4 values like border-radius. Superellipse exponent must be > 0. Interacts with border-radius (no radius = no effect), border-image (shapes follow shape), and clip-path (corner-clip applies ...

openQuestions.title: Cover at least one failure mode
openQuestions.detail: The page currently shows the happy path. Add a UI affordance that drives the feature into a failure mode the spec explicitly calls out (timeout, denied permission, cross-origin block, malformed input) and surface what the browser actually does.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus reference link
openQuestions.detail: Every concept page must cite chromestatus.com/feature/<id> in its 'see also' or references section — this is the self-heal anchor used by the slug cleanup script.

openQuestions.title: Tighten the design-system compliance
openQuestions.detail: Either replace raw hex with --bg-*/--text-*/--accent-* tokens, or pair every button background override with a color override (the global button rule sets both — overriding one alone risks light-on-light).

openQuestions.title: Reconcile blurb and page contents
openQuestions.detail: The feature index blurb promises behaviours that the page doesn't yet exercise. Either trim the blurb to match what the page does, or add the missing UI.

summary: Concept needs follow-up work before it really lands. 5 open question(s) queued.
